name: Ibrahim ibn Muslim
title: Stranger Child of Kufa
shortDescription: One of the two children of Muslim ibn Aqeel who was taken captive after Ashura and ultimately unjustly martyred by the cruel-hearted men of Kufa.
fullStory:
Ibrahim ibn Muslim was one of the two young sons of Muslim ibn Aqeel (Imam Hussain's ambassador) famously known as 'Tiflan-e-Muslim' (The Two Children of Muslim). He was in the Karbala caravan and had tasted the grief of his father's martyrdom before Ashura.

After the event of Ashura, Ibrahim and his younger brother Muhammad got lost during the looting of the tents and were subsequently captured and imprisoned by Ibn Ziyad's forces. They spent about a year in a dark prison in Kufa.

Eventually, with the help of an old jailer who was a Shia, they escaped, but at night took refuge in the house of a woman whose son-in-law (Harith) was one of Ibn Ziyad's loyalists. Harith noticed their presence in the middle of the night and brutally arrested them to get the reward.

He took these two orphaned children to the banks of the Euphrates River. Ibrahim and his brother cried and begged him not to sell them or give them as slaves, but the cruel-hearted Harith severed both oppressed children's heads and threw their bodies into the Euphrates.
quoteArabic: يا شَيْخُ! اِرْحَمْ صِغَرَ سِنِّنا وَغُرْبَتَنا...
quote: (They told Harith at the time of martyrdom:) O old man! Have mercy on our young age and our strangerhood, and do not kill us...
